rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim is internally conflicted: the method is named 'Adaptive Temporal Attention' and framed around attention-budget gating, but the core formulation (h_t = A·h_{t-1} + B·x_t) is a standard SSM recurrence indistinguishable from S4/Mamba. Improving the draft requires resolving or at least clarifying this conceptual framing.
rationale.R1.2: The logical arc breaks at the pivot between paragraphs 1 and 2: the paper promises an attention mechanism with learnable gating, then delivers an SSM without explaining the relationship. 'Improve' plausibly requires fixing this inferential gap and ensuring the ablation conclusions (selectivity = principal driver; dense A = no gain) are internally consistent with the model description.
rationale.R1.3: The empirical paragraph makes several claims—ETTh1 MSE 0.412 vs. 0.439, 6% ablation degradation, improved cross-horizon generalization—without specifying horizon baselines, variance, or where Section 5 evidence appears. Improving the draft plausibly includes tightening claim-to-evidence traceability.
rationale.R1.6: A generic 'improve this draft' delegation always activates writing polish as at least a central expected responsibility; sentence-level clarity, paragraph cohesion, and precision of technical language are all improvable in the current text.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvement focus primarily on prose and writing clarity, or on resolving the technical/logical issues—specifically the framing inconsistency between the 'attention mechanism' framing in the name and introduction and the SSM recurrence that constitutes the actual model?
